response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web-platform engineer with over 15 years of experience, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The most critical disqualifier is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency — a stated hard requirement — as his languages are English, Romanian, and German. Beyond that, his background is almost entirely in web, SaaS, and data-pipeline work (TypeScript, Node.js, MongoDB, React), with no demonstrable expertise in network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), Layer-2/3 switching, Linux kernel modification, or embedded firmware development. His brief C/C++ exposure at National Instruments in 2014–2015 is too dated and too shallow to compensate for these gaps.
strengths: Long track record of software craftsmanship including TDD, CI/CD, and clean architecture across diverse stacks
Genuine embedded and low-latency systems exposure (DLang async I/O, robotic-arm work, Sound Position Server with Linux/Jack/C++)
REST API and Linux familiarity provides a thin foundation that is at least directionally relevant to the networking management plane
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this role
No security domain knowledge whatsoever: no MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption key exchange, or attack/tamper detection experience
No network switching or Layer-2/3 protocol experience, and no Linux kernel source-level work evidenced anywhere in the resume

key_factors.factor: No security or L2/L3 networking experience
No Linux kernel or embedded firmware experience
